compiled_state.title: As a developer, ticket-graph.py exposes a hierarchy resolver that finds the effective link target for any ticket pair
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
**What**: Add resolve_hierarchy_link(source_id, target_id, tracker_dir) to ticket-graph.py. Walks each ticket's parent_id chain (≤2 hops) using the existing _reduce_ticket_state() mechanism, finds the divergence point in the hierarchy, and returns (resolved_source_id, resolved_target_id, was_redirected, is_redundant). Expose as CLI subcommand python3 ticket-graph.py resolve-hierarchy-link <src> <tgt> with JSON stdout for shell integration.
**Why**: The foundational library that add_dependency() will call; testable in isolation before integration.
**Scope**:
- IN: resolve_hierarchy_link() function, CLI subcommand resolve-hierarchy-link, orphan handling (no parent_id → pass through), explicit error on missing/unreadable ticket state (not silent orphan fallthrough), JSON IPC contract {resolved_source, resolved_target, was_redirected, is_redundant}
- OUT: Integration into add_dependency() (S2), cycle detection (S3), benchmark tests (S4)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, calling resolve-hierarchy-link on two tasks sharing the same parent story returns the original IDs unchanged with was_redirected: false, is_redundant: false
  ← Satisfies: SC1 (same-parent-story task pair allowed)
- When this story is complete, calling it on cross-story same-epic task pairs returns the two parent story IDs as the resolved pair with was_redirected: true
  ← Satisfies: SC3 (cross-story same-epic → story-story)
- When this story is complete, calling it on cross-epic task pairs returns the two root epic IDs with was_redirected: true
  ← Satisfies: SC5 (cross-epic → epic-epic)
- When this story is complete, a ticket with no parent_id returns original IDs unchanged with was_redirected: false, is_redundant: false
  ← Satisfies: SC10 (orphan tickets)
- When this story is complete, if ticket state cannot be reduced for any ticket in the ancestry chain, the function exits non-zero with a JSON error object rather than silently treating as orphan
  ← Satisfies: SC11
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Implementation] Must use existing _reduce_ticket_state() for parent_id lookups — NOT read .cache.json directly. ticket-graph.py explicitly skips .cache.json during state reduction; diverging causes data inconsistencies.
- [Testing] Unit tests must use mock ticket state; integration tests deferred to S4
- [Maintainability] JSON output format is the IPC contract for S2 — define and document it in this story before S2 begins

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: COMPLEXITY_CLASSIFICATION: TRIVIAL — skipping /dso:implementation-plan, story dispatched directly as task
comments.author: Test
